description: §52.15/§52.8 — an auth-scoped Tier-1 server-authority cell (`authority="server"` → always an UNSCOPED SELECT *) under <program auth="required"> fires W-SSR-PRERENDER-UNSCOPED (warning). Its SSR pre-render bakes EVERY user's rows into the first-paint HTML, served identically to all viewers — a cross-user leak the client-fetch path does not have.
rationale: §52.15: a Tier-1 authority cell's SELECT * is unscoped; when it is auth-scoped (the app is auth-aware and the route is not explicitly public) its unscoped SSR pre-render leaks every row across users. Row-scope it to @currentUser.id (§52.6.5) to fix. SEVERITY ESCALATION (ss60 W2): fires only a WARNING — PA/user to rule whether a cross-user first-paint leak should be a freeze-blocking ERROR. DIVERGENCE FROM LIST: the ss60 W2 note anticipated this code being converted to an info-lint by an in-flight SSR fix; that fix was HELD (commit 8cb00161), so the LIVE severity is still `warning` — authored against live state.
